response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong polyglot engineer with solid distributed-systems and event-driven architecture experience, but the role's core requirement — deep, sustained C++ expertise — is nearly absent from his career. His only C++ work appears in a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor-thesis audio project; everything since has been TypeScript, JavaScript, Node.js, and DLang. He shows no exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443 secure-development standards, MQTT, or any of the industrial protocols (OPC UA, Modbus, DNP 3.0) listed as advantageous, making him a poor fit for this role despite his breadth in other areas.
strengths: Proven high-throughput distributed-systems experience (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency design) maps loosely to the scalability and concurrency demands of the role.
Demonstrated event-driven and message-broker-style architecture in GISCollective (open-source, Kubernetes-deployable geo-data platform) aligns with the message-broker and REST/IoT requirements.
Strong software-craftsmanship ethos (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code katas, mentoring workshops) matches the quality-mindset and mentoring behavioral competencies.
concerns: C++ proficiency is negligible — only 6 months of C/C++ work at NI (Dec 2014–Jun 2015); the entire subsequent decade is TypeScript/JavaScript/Node.js/DLang, making him unqualified for a role where C++ and STL mastery are the primary technical requirement.

Zero domain exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, or any industrial-cybersecurity framework, which are central to both the responsibilities and the security-expertise section of the JD.
Educational background (BS Computer Science + Fine Arts degree) does not meet the B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech engineering-degree requirement specified for this position.
